campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth with recreation scholarships. They also discussed plans to build a safer bike and pedestrian crossing over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, and voted on extending a temporary local rule that had been put in place urgently.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donating $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
Hamilton Avenue bike/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 - applying for grant funding
Final feasibility study completed for the Hamilton Avenue crossing project
Extension of interim ordinance #2326 (temporary local rule)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide which community groups and events would receive grant money from the city for the upcoming fiscal year. They approved funding for selected organizations, set specific dollar amounts for each recipient, and made updates to the rules for how groups can apply for and use these event grants.
keyTopics: Approved which organizations get city grant funding and how much each receives
Finalized the Competitive Event Grant Program budget for the fiscal year
Updated the Community Event and Street Closure Grant rules for clarity

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the proceedings. The council offered multiple ways to tune in, including live translations in over 50 languages for those who need them.
keyTopics: Meeting access via cable TV (Channel 26), city website, and YouTube
Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Phone dial-in option for residents without internet
Online Zoom participation available

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council also reviewed reports from various committees and discussed upcoming meetings for city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Upcoming board and commission meeting schedules
Recreation Division contact for agenda questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community programs in Sunnyvale. The group approved meeting notes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking this funding to support local housing and services.
keyTopics: Federal grant applications for affordable housing projects
Community development funding decisions
Approval of February meeting minutes
Local organizations pitching for housing and services funding

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses thrive by launching a program that forgives back business licenses for shops that come forward, reaching out to shopping centers to participate. They're also planning summer events to encourage residents to shop locally and exploring ways to partner with private companies to sponsor city events and offer financial incentives for new retail stores and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - allows businesses to get current on licenses without penalties
Shopping center outreach - targeting malls and strip centers to boost participation
Summer Shop Local campaign - promoting Cupertino businesses through seasonal marketing
Private-public partnerships for city event sponsorships
Financial incentives for new retail and startup businesses

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a Governance and Ethics Committee meeting on March 30, 2026. The city is continuing to offer both in-person attendance and remote participation options so residents can attend however works best for them.
keyTopics: Meeting held in-person with remote participation available
Zoom and phone options provided for residents unable to attend in person
Public comment submission available via email to mayorandcouncil@santaclaraca.gov by 10 a.m. before meetings
Governance and Ethics Committee discussions

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to recognize Women's History Month and approve two main items: a disaster response agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities when emergencies happen, and a three-year contract to buy email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster facility agreement
Email security software purchase ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
